original_query: SELECT "t0"."JOB" FROM (SELECT "EMPNO", "ENAME", "JOB", "MGR", "HIREDATE", "SAL", "COMM", "DEPTNO", "SLACKER", "DEPTNO" + "EMPNO" AS "f9" FROM "EMP" WHERE "ENAME" = 'A') AS "t0" FULL JOIN (SELECT "DEPTNO", "NAME", "DEPTNO" + 5 AS "f2" FROM "DEPT") AS "t1" ON "t0"."JOB" = "t1"."NAME" AND "t0"."f9" = "t1"."f2" GROUP BY "t0"."JOB"
rewritten_query:
SELECT "EMP"."JOB"
FROM "EMP"
LEFT JOIN "DEPT"
  ON "EMP"."JOB" = "DEPT"."NAME"
  AND ("EMP"."DEPTNO" + "EMP"."EMPNO") = ("DEPT"."DEPTNO" + 5)
WHERE "EMP"."ENAME" = 'A'
GROUP BY "EMP"."JOB"

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Changed FULL JOIN to LEFT JOIN since the query is grouping by t0.JOB from the EMP table, making the FULL JOIN unnecessary and inefficient
Eliminated unnecessary subqueries by moving the calculations directly into the join condition
Simplified the query structure by removing the derived tables while maintaining the same filtering logic
